metadata.title: Coffee Water Science — Structured Data
metadata.description: Complete structured data for water chemistry, SCA standards, mineral profiles, filtration systems, and water recipes for coffee brewing

sources.source: Hendon, C.H. et al. (2014). Role of dissolved cations in coffee extraction. JACS.
Colonna-Dashwood, M. & Hendon, C. (2015). Water for Coffee.

parameter.name: Chlorine
parameter.note: Must be zero — use carbon filtration

parameter.name: Chloramine
parameter.note: Must be zero — requires catalytic carbon

notes.note: Water outside these parameters should be treated before use
Distilled or reverse osmosis water must be remineralised
High chlorine destroys coffee volatile aromatics

mineral.name: Magnesium
mineral.sca_note: Most effective extraction mineral per unit
mineral.extraction_role: Forms stronger complexes with coffee flavor compounds (chlorogenic acids, caffeine, melanoidins) due to smaller ionic radius and higher charge density relative to size. Significantly more effective than calcium at extracting desirable flavor compounds.
mineral.source_compound: Magnesium Sulfate (MgSO4·7H2O — Epsom Salt) for remineralisation
mineral.research_reference: Hendon et al. (2014) JACS — demonstrated Mg2+ extracts significantly more flavor per unit than Ca2+

mineral.name: Calcium
mineral.extraction_role: Contributes to hardness; aids extraction; provides body and structure to cup
mineral.scale_risk: HIGH — forms calcium carbonate (CaCO3) scale at high temperature
mineral.source_compound: Calcium Chloride (CaCl2) or Calcium Sulfate (CaSO4) for remineralisation

mineral.name: Bicarbonate
mineral.extraction_role: Primary buffer — neutralises acids in coffee. Too high = flat, dull cup (acids neutralised). Too low = sharp, sour cup (no buffering). Target maintains acid balance.
mineral.source_compound: Sodium Bicarbonate (NaHCO3) or Potassium Bicarbonate (KHCO3) for remineralisation

mineral.name: Sodium
mineral.extraction_role: Minor extraction role; low amounts enhance sweetness perception
mineral.concern: Ion exchange softeners raise sodium by replacing Ca2+/Mg2+ — not recommended for specialty coffee flavor

mineral.name: Potassium
mineral.extraction_role: Minor; similar to sodium in small amounts; some evidence of flavor enhancement
mineral.note: Used in some advanced water recipes (Aqua Magica) as alkalinity source with less Na+ impact

mineral.name: Chloride
mineral.extraction_role: Enhances sweetness at low levels; can enhance extraction efficiency
mineral.note: Different from Chlorine (Cl2) — chloride ion is the anion from calcium chloride remineralisation, not the disinfectant

mineral.name: Sulfate
mineral.extraction_role: Accentuates bitterness and dryness at higher levels; provides counterion for Mg2+ in remineralisation
mineral.note: Present when using Epsom Salt (MgSO4) as magnesium source. Higher sulfate-to-chloride ratio = more bitter/dry character (borrowed from brewing water science)

class.name: Soft
class.description: Low mineral content; risk of flat extraction; corrosive to equipment

class.name: Moderately Soft
class.description: Good for coffee; low scale risk

class.name: Very Hard
class.description: Significant scale risk; full treatment required

hardness_classification.hardness_formula: Total Hardness (as CaCO3) = (Ca²⁺ × 2.497) + (Mg²⁺ × 4.118) Where Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺ are in mg/L

recipe.name: SCA Benchmark Water
recipe.best_for: Filter coffee, pour-over, AeroPress; general SCA-standard brewing

recipe.name: Barista Hustle Aqua Magica (Concentrate)
recipe.best_for: Specialty espresso; competition; bright filter coffees

recipe.name: Espresso Optimised Water
recipe.best_for: Espresso where body and crema are priority; milk-based drinks

system.name: Activated Carbon Block Filter
system.removes: Chlorine, chloramine, VOCs, sediment, taste/odour compounds
system.coffee_suitability: Essential minimum for any coffee application

system.name: Template Assisted Crystallisation (TAC) Scale Inhibitor
system.mechanism: Converts dissolved Ca/Mg ions to micro-crystals that cannot adhere to boiler/pipe surfaces
system.coffee_suitability: Excellent — preserves brewing minerals while protecting equipment

system.name: Reverse Osmosis (RO)
system.mechanism: Semi-permeable membrane at 40-80 PSI rejects 90-99% of dissolved minerals
system.removes: 90-99% of all dissolved minerals, bacteria, heavy metals
system.coffee_suitability: Requires remineralisation — pure RO water is corrosive and produces flat extraction
system.waste_ratio: 2-4 litres waste per litre product water
system.best_for: Very hard water; competition preparation; precise water control

system.name: Ion Exchange Softener
system.mechanism: Resin beads exchange Ca2+/Mg2+ for Na+ ions
system.coffee_suitability: POOR for flavor — removes beneficial Mg2+; raises sodium above SCA limit. Use only for machine protection, with separate flavor-grade filter for brew water

system.name: BWT Bestmax System (Commercial Standard)
system.mechanism: Multi-stage: Mg-enriched ion exchange + activated carbon + TAC
system.unique_feature: Replaces Ca2+ with Mg2+ (beneficial exchange — improves extraction vs standard softener)
system.coffee_suitability: Excellent — industry standard for specialty cafés

scale_science.scale_reaction: Ca²⁺ + 2HCO₃⁻ → CaCO₃↓ + H₂O + CO₂ (at elevated temperature)

scale_impact.impact: 0.1mm scale = approximately 10% more energy to heat boiler
Blocks flow restrictors and group head screens

scale_science.warning: Never use vinegar on espresso machines — acetic acid corrodes brass fittings and O-rings

tool.name: TDS/EC Pen (HM Digital)
tool.notes: Approximation via conductivity; not as precise as refractometer

tool.name: pH Meter (Apera AI209)
tool.notes: Calibrate with buffer solutions before each use

tool.name: ICP-MS Lab Analysis
tool.notes: Third-party lab; most accurate; best for competition or precision setup

water_physical_properties.notes: Lower viscosity at higher temperature = water flows more easily through puck; affects grind setting. Lower surface tension = better pore wetting = more even puck saturation.
